architecture.summary: SSI-Policy is a modular vision-language robotic manipulation framework centered on a Structured Scene Interface (SSI)—an RGB-only intermediate representation that jointly encodes monocular depth features, language-grounded object layouts, and instruction-conditioned 2D motion trajectories. The SSI is trained from action-free video independent of the robot embodiment, and a downstream policy learns control from few demonstrations by reading from this decoupled geometric and semantic representation. Information flows: RGB video → SSI encoder (depth + object layout + motion trajectories) → policy decoder (action predictions).
trained_components: SSI encoder (monocular depth, object layout, motion trajectory modules)

biblio.title: SSI-Policy: Learning Structured Scene Interfaces for Vision-Language Robotic Manipulation

claims.statement: On the LIBERO benchmark with only 10 demonstrations per task, SSI-Policy improves over the strongest prior method by nearly 15%
SSI-Policy remains competitive with 50-demo methods that leverage large-scale external pretraining
Geometric and motion cues provide complementary benefits within the shared SSI interface
SSI is robot-agnostic and trainable from action-free video, decoupling perception from control so that the downstream policy can learn from few demonstrations
Validation on 13 real-world tasks spanning spatial reasoning, cross-embodiment transfer, and contact-rich manipulation
